text:
There once was a village in the valley nobody visited. People said it was the end of nowhere, a shadow. At the edge of this shadow lived a grandmother named Olive. She had silver hair and eyes like pools of twilight.

One evening, after her usual long walk home along the river, Olive came upon a clearing she'd never seen before. Sitting atop an aged oak was a wise old owl. Olive sat beside him, listening. He was telling tales. One was of a traveler who crossed a bridge into the End-of-Nothing.

The traveler's journey had been fraught with challenges. He was tired. Every step felt like walking through fog. But finally, after countless hours, he arrived at the bridge. It shimmered under the moonlight. The traveler looked across. There were no houses, no shops. Nothing.

The old owl nodded sagely. "There is indeed nothing but the void between things. But if you cross, you become immortal. One can't get lost in
